title: Add prompt caching support to Anthropic client
description:
Implement Anthropic prompt caching (cache_control) in internal/model/anthropic/client.go to reduce costs and latency for agentic tool loops.

## Background
Each turn in the agentic loop resends the entire conversation. Without caching, you pay full input token price every time. With caching:
- Cache writes: 1.25x base cost
- Cache reads: 0.1x base cost (90% savings on cached content)

## Implementation
1. Add cache_control field to apiContent struct
2. Mark stable prefixes with cache_control: {type: 'ephemeral'}:
   - End of tools array
   - End of system prompt  
   - End of conversation history (before new user message)
3. Track cache metrics from response (cache_creation_input_tokens, cache_read_input_tokens)

## Considerations
- 5-minute TTL by default (refreshed on use)
- Minimum cacheable: 1024-4096 tokens depending on model
- Cache hierarchy: tools → system → messages
- Up to 4 cache breakpoints allowed

## References
- https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/build-with-claude/prompt-caching

title: Add context caching support to Gemini client
description:
Implement Gemini context caching in internal/model/gemini/client.go to reduce costs for agentic tool loops.

## Background
Gemini offers two caching mechanisms:
1. **Implicit caching** - automatic, no setup (enabled May 2025)
2. **Explicit caching** - manual CachedContent objects for guaranteed savings

For agentic loops, explicit caching gives more control over what's cached.

## Gemini vs Anthropic Approach
Unlike Anthropic's inline `cache_control` markers, Gemini uses a separate resource model:
- Create a `CachedContent` object with system instructions, files, etc.
- Reference it by ID in subsequent requests via `cached_content` parameter
- Manage lifecycle (list, update TTL, delete)

## Implementation
1. Add CachedContent creation/management to Gemini client
2. Cache stable content (system prompt, tools, large context)
3. Reference cached content in generation requests
4. Track cache for cleanup/TTL refresh

## Considerations
- Default TTL: 1 hour (configurable)
- Minimum cacheable: 1,024 tokens (Flash) / 4,096 tokens (Pro)
- Cache is a separate API resource (not inline like Anthropic)
- Need to manage cache lifecycle across sessions

## References
- https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/caching

title: Eval: Add conditional turn support for multi-turn tests
description:
Add support for conditional turns in multi-turn eval tests. This would allow turn 2 to only execute if turn 1 met certain conditions (e.g., coordinator asked for clarification).

## Problem
Currently, multi-turn tests always execute all turns sequentially. When testing behaviors with variable LLM responses (e.g., coordinator may OR may not ask for clarification), the follow-up turn may not make sense if the previous turn took a different path.

## Example
- Turn 1: User asks about config, coordinator MAY ask 'which file?' OR answer directly
- Turn 2: User says 'config.yaml' (only makes sense if turn 1 asked for clarification)

## Proposed Solution
Add a `condition` field to turns:
```yaml
turns:
  - user: 'Is debug mode enabled?'
    assert:
      - type: asked-clarification
        optional: true
  - user: 'config.yaml'
    condition:
      previous_assertion: asked-clarification
      result: passed
    assert:
      - type: llm-rubric
        rubric: 'Response identifies debug is enabled'
```

## Files
- internal/eval/runner_v2.go - Add conditional turn logic
- internal/eval/types.go - Add Condition field to ConversationTurn

title: Add prompt caching support to OpenAI/ZAI client
description:
Implement OpenAI prompt caching options in internal/model/zai/client.go to optimize costs and latency for agentic tool loops.

## Background
OpenAI's prompt caching is automatic - no code changes required for basic caching. However, there are optional parameters to optimize cache behavior:
- `prompt_cache_retention`: 'in_memory' (default) or '24h' (gpt-5.x models)
- `prompt_cache_key`: Influence routing for better hit rates

## OpenAI vs Anthropic/Gemini Approach
OpenAI is fully automatic (routing-based hashing), unlike:
- Anthropic: Inline `cache_control` markers
- Gemini: Explicit `CachedContent` resource objects

## Implementation
1. Verify automatic caching works with current ZAI client (should 'just work')
2. Add optional `prompt_cache_retention` parameter support ('in_memory' | '24h')
3. Add optional `prompt_cache_key` parameter for routing optimization
4. Track cache metrics from response (cached_tokens in usage)
5. Structure prompts to maximize cache hits (stable prefixes first)

## Considerations
- Automatic caching enabled by default (no setup needed)
- Minimum cacheable: 1,024 tokens
- Default TTL: 5-10 min inactive, max 1 hour
- Extended TTL (24h): Available for gpt-5.x models
- Cache eviction is automatic (no manual clear)
- Up to 90% cost reduction, 80% latency reduction on hits

## References
- https://developers.openai.com/api/docs/guides/prompt-caching/
